title: Cookies, Credential Modes, API Keys, Bearer Tokens, and Secret Hygiene · Browser and Node Client Systems · Compiler Case: Api Key Placement
summary: A browser client selects omit, same-origin, or include credentials and separates cookies, CSRF, API keys, and bearer-token authority. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts api key placement increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch least-authority credential and session policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Place API keys in approved headers or signing inputs and exclude them from URLs, source, telemetry, and error text. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Recognize ambient cookie authority and require CSRF defenses for state-changing browser requests. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
